$schema_note: Conforms to ADR-025 + ADR-026; inherits shape from cycle_101_d11_og_cards_regen.json (1st canonical instance).

step_1_capture_state.target_surface: site/src/assets/heroes/ (NEW directory; 4 hero PNGs for /learn /how /patterns /reference section landing pages)
step_1_capture_state.method: agentic discovery of current section index pages + DocumentationLayout props

pre_change_inventory.current_hero_assets_in_site: site/src/assets/aDNABanner.png (home page only; 1288x512 pixel-art wordmark on metal plate) + site/src/assets/banner.jpg (legacy)
pre_change_inventory.section_index_hero_state:
NONE — learn/how/patterns/reference/community index.astro pages have only

intro + CardGrid; no hero imagery
pre_change_inventory.documentation_layout_hero_support: absent — Props interface = {title, description, headings, prev, next, jsonLD}; heroImage prop not yet defined

step_1_capture_state.scope_decision: cycle 102 generates 4 hero ASSETS only (learn + how + patterns + reference; 4 highest-distinct sections); layout wiring (DocumentationLayout heroImage prop extension + per-section index.astro consumer) deferred to S2 cycle 104-106 per M5.0 §3 D11 per-cycle assignment table (cycle 5-6 = section icon integration; cycle 102 = asset generation only). Community + use-cases heroes deferred to cycle 104 (would have brought total to 6 per M5.0 spec).

step_2_persona_qa.method: agent-simulated (Q&A focus per cycle 102 mission spec: Q1 Visual Style + Q5 Explanation Quality per M5.1 D11 LIFT-1 philosophy-before-feature gravity well)
step_2_persona_qa.qa_protocol_focus: Q1 + Q5 emphasized per M5.3 mission spec cycle 102 scope (philosophy-before-feature minimalism is the central design tension; hero is the most visible carrier of that posture)
step_2_persona_qa.design_question_at_cycle_entry: Should section hero match cycle-101 OG card aesthetic (consistency) or differentiate at landing-page-scale (variety)?

persona_consensus.design_critic: Match. Section hero + OG card form a brand system — the OG card is a 1200x630 preview of what the user sees on the section landing page. Mismatching would break recognition. Match motif vocabulary; widen to 16:9 hero band.
persona_consensus.visual_designer: Match. Consistency wins for a methodology project (vs. variety for a consumer product). Per-section motif (paths / hexagons / blueprint / workflow) carries across both surfaces without becoming repetitive because each section reads its own visual story.
persona_consensus.newcomer_stress_tester: Match. When I clicked through from an OG-preview share to the actual page, I want the visual to confirm I'm in the right place — recognition signal beats variety surprise.
persona_consensus.accessibility_auditor: Match. Consistency reduces cognitive load (Cognitive_Load dimension; one of the 4 NEW M5.0 secondary). Variety would require relearning visual vocabulary on each page entry.

pre_change_dimension_aggregate_scores.visual_clarity: N/A — pre-change has no hero asset at section pages

step_2_persona_qa.qa_synthesis: 4/4 personas converge on match-cycle-101-motif-vocabulary. Cycle 102 generates 16:9 wide hero bands using the same per-section motif language as cycle 101 OG cards (learn=paths, how=workflow, patterns=hexagons, reference=blueprint). Restraint discipline carries forward: dark teal palette + single accent + abstract-geometric + no text + no human figures + no marketing-style.

step_3_generate_images.regen_reason: N/A — cycle 102 prompts inherit the hex-code-stripped discipline from cycle 101 regen pass; first pass clean (no text bleed in any of 4 cards). Validates skill_imagen_prompt_text_stripping pattern at 2nd canonical instance (1/3 → 2/3).
step_3_generate_images.auth_method: SS_GEMINI_API_KEY from macOS Keychain via /usr/bin/security (non-TTY-safe; same as cycle 101)
step_3_generate_images.prompt_discipline: Per-section motif sentence + shared restraint tail. Hero-specific adjustment vs OG cards: motif fills full width (no 'left half quiet' instruction); text overlay handled by Astro layout (not PIL) so no compositing step. Per-section motifs match cycle 101 vocabulary: layered geometric paths (learn); workflow arrows curving between rectangular nodes (how); tessellating hexagons (patterns); technical blueprint grid (reference).
step_3_generate_images.approach_validated:
Pure Imagen Ultra at 16:9 native (no resize/crop needed since Imagen returns 1408x768 ~~ 1408x792 target); no PIL text compositing for heroes (text overlay = Astro + sibling

/

elements at layout level, not baked into image). Output: clean 4/4.

step_4_implement.method: Runner script writes 4 PNGs to site/src/assets/heroes/{learn,how,patterns,reference}.png. No Astro layout changes this cycle (deferred to S2 cycle 104-106).
step_4_implement.astro_build_skipped: Per AC #10 — cycle 102 is pure asset generation (no .astro template or .ts component changes); next Astro build will discover the new asset paths via standard Vite resolution when consumer pages import them. No build verification at this commit; verification deferred to cycle 106 (wiring cycle).

step_5_recapture.method: Visual Read tool review of all 4 generated hero PNGs at full resolution.

files_size_summary.size_concern_note: Per-hero ~835 KB; consumer-side <Picture> with avif/webp formats + responsive widths (per existing aDNABanner.png pattern in site/src/pages/index.astro) will compress these to ~150-250 KB final delivery. Acceptable trade-off.

step_6_validate.method: Agent-simulated persona scoring on the 4 new hero assets (without yet seeing them rendered in layout context — pure asset-level scoring; layout-integration rescoring at cycle 106).

post_change_dimension_scores.findability: N/A — heroes not yet wired into layout; findability scoring at cycle 106 post-integration

persona_specific_post_change_notes.design_critic: All 4 heroes maintain the cycle-101 motif vocabulary cleanly. hero_reference (blueprint grid) is the strongest visual — densest geometric activity. hero_how (workflow arrows) is the most narrative — flows left-to-right which suits a documentation hero band. hero_learn + hero_patterns are clean but lighter-density (intentional restraint). Score 4.5/5.0 on Visual_Clarity.
persona_specific_post_change_notes.visual_designer: Per-section accent colors register cleanly even at wide aspect — cyan/teal-green/amber-gold/deep-teal each distinct. Tone discipline preserved across all 4 (no off-palette colors, no aspirational glow effects). Score 4.5/5.0.
persona_specific_post_change_notes.newcomer_stress_tester: If I clicked through from an OG share to the section page (e.g., OG-learn → /learn), seeing hero_learn with matching motif at the top would confirm I'm in the right place. Recognition signal works. Score 4.0/5.0 on Cognitive_Load.
persona_specific_post_change_notes.accessibility_auditor: Hero assets themselves are non-textual (good — text overlay handled by Astro layout at WCAG-tested CSS); contrast within image is fine (geometric forms ~3-5:1 vs background, but these are decorative not informational so WCAG doesn't apply). Score 4.5/5.0 on Visual_Clarity.

lift_avoid_verification.m51_d11_lift_1_philosophy_before_feature: HONORED — all 4 heroes are abstract geometric compositions; no aspirational imagery; no product mockups; no human-figure scenes.
lift_avoid_verification.m51_d11_avoid_2_marketing_style_hero_ima: AVOIDED — no photographic stock-photo aesthetic; no over-saturated colors; no aspirational compositions. Heroes read as architectural/technical, not promotional. Especially hero_how (workflow arrows) and hero_reference (blueprint) — these read as documentation infrastructure, not marketing decoration.

step_6_validate.cycle_acceptance: PASS — 4/4 heroes clean (no text bleed; restraint posture honored; per-section motif vocabulary matches cycle 101 OG cards); budget within cap ($0.16 of $1.50 S1 cap, cumulative S1 = $0.52). Wiring deferred to S2 cycle 104-106 per scope decision.

step_7_record_iii_result.schema_conformance_adr_025_026: Conforms; inherits shape from cycle 101 JSON (1st canonical instance). Cycles 103-110 continue this shape.

lift_carried_forward_to_cycle_103: per-section motif vocabulary works across aspect ratios (16:9 hero vs 1200x630 OG card vs square icon); cycle 103 section icons can follow same motif language at icon scale
avoid_carried_forward: no text in image (universal Imagen discipline; carries through cycle 103+ unless icon explicitly needs glyph-text per accessibility)
no hex codes in prompts (validated at 2nd canonical instance; skill_imagen_prompt_text_stripping advances 1/3 → 2/3)
lift_avoid_routing.skill_imagen_background_plus_pil_text_ov: 1/3 → still 1/3 (cycle 102 did NOT use the two-pass pattern — heroes are pure Imagen without text overlay; this pattern applies specifically to text-bearing surfaces like OG cards, not text-free heroes)
lift_avoid_routing.skill_imagen_prompt_text_stripping_advan: 1/3 → 2/3 (cycle 101 1st instance with regen-after-failure; cycle 102 2nd instance with clean-first-pass via inherited discipline)
